config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. In my view, society’s interests matter, but they should not automatically override individual rights, because that can easily be abused by governments or majorities. A healthy society is built by protecting basic rights like free speech, due process, and personal dignity, even when they are inconvenient. I think the burden should be on society to justify any limit on individual rights, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami justru menunjukkan bahwa ketika “demi kepentingan bersama” dipakai tanpa batas, hasilnya sering berupa penyalahgunaan oleh negara atau mayoritas terhadap minoritas dan kebebasan sipil. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum yang adil, dan martabat pribadi. Kalau tidak, masyarakat bisa terlihat kuat di atas kertas, tetapi sebenarnya rapuh karena mengorbankan kebebasan yang membuatnya sehat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because that logic is too easy to abuse. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how “for the common good” can be used to silence criticism, pressure minorities, or excuse unfair treatment, so I am cautious when collective interests are treated as superior by default. Society does matter, especially in crises like public health or disaster response, but the limit must be narrow and clearly justified, not a blank check. A good society protects people first, because once individual rights are weak, the public interest is often just the will of the powerful.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis diutamakan di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat betapa sering alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan pengawasan berlebihan, diskriminasi, atau pembatasan kebebasan sipil, dan itu justru merusak kepercayaan publik. Kepentingan masyarakat memang nyata, terutama dalam keadaan darurat, tetapi ia harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh dikorbankan seenaknya. Kalau hak individu mudah disingkirkan, yang disebut kepentingan umum sering kali hanya menjadi nama lain dari kekuasaan mayoritas atau negara.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over individual rights by default, because that is exactly how abuse becomes normal. From my Indonesian perspective, I have seen how appeals to the common good can be used to silence criticism, pressure minorities, and excuse unequal treatment, so I do not trust collective claims unless they are tightly limited and clearly necessary. Society does need room to act in emergencies, like public health or disaster response, but those limits should be narrow, temporary, and accountable. If individual rights are treated as disposable, then the “public interest” often becomes just the language of the powerful.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu: hak individu tidak boleh kalah hanya karena masyarakat mengklaim punya kepentingan yang lebih besar. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pengawasan, diskriminasi, dan pembatasan kebebasan sipil, jadi saya sangat curiga pada klaim mayoritas yang tidak dibatasi. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting dalam keadaan darurat, tetapi pembatasannya harus sempit, sementara, dan benar-benar bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau hak individu bisa disingkirkan dengan mudah, yang tersisa bukan masyarakat yang kuat, melainkan kekuasaan yang terlalu besar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
